reviews.review_text: i live 30 minutes from this emergency room but still go here anytime i need to get checked out because they always have excellent care & service here. unfortunately my last visit on Friday morning did not go well at all. Enough for me to feel like i needed to write this review. I was see by Dr Kristine Mascarinas. She was no help whatsoever and it genuinely felt like she didn’t care at all to try to help either. I was feeling so bad i couldn’t speak so my mom had to speak for me and all i can hear is the doctor asking my mom what does she want her to do. My mom is not the doctor. Dr Kristine Mascarinas is. They got bloodwork from me just to not even run any tests on me at all. She was very rude and her vibe was completely off putting. I didn’t feel safe or cared for under her care honestly one of the worst doctors i’ve had. Later on a male doctor came and I wish i got his name. he was awesome, kind, understanding, and empathetic to my situation. I then felt cared for. I hope this review gets back to Dr Kristine Mascarinas because she really needs to do a better job.
reviews.owner_answer: Hello Ally - Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. We truly value your feedback and will be sure to look into your concerns. If you would like to speak to a member of our Patient Experience team, please email us at PatientExperience@baptisthealth.net or call 786-596-3750.

reviews.review_text: Quick and excellent service. From the moment I arrived, I gave them my information, they took me to the room, and the doctor came. I felt very well taken care of. Thank you, and God bless you all for your excellent work.

reviews.review_text:
The Baptist Emergency Center at Coral Way and 135th Avenue SW has a special distinction: the professionalism and compassion of its medical and administrative staff. It was my first visit to the center, and I thought it was a great idea to serve the community without having to go to the hospital's main emergency room. Dr. Martinez, who saw me, along with the ultrasound technician Alina and the young nursing assistant, provided me with truly high-quality medical services, compassion, and great professionalism. Congratulations to all of them.

José Rodolfo Alfonso
reviews.original_review_text:
En centro de Emergencia de Baptist de Coral Way y 135 Avenida del
S.W. posee una especial singularidad y es el profesionalismo y carácter humano de su personal médico y staff de oficina. Era mi primera visita al centro y me pareció muy buena idea de servir a la comunidad  sin tener que ir a la emergencia Central en el hospital . El Dr. Martínez, quien me atendió
Al igual que la técnica de ultrasonidos Alina así como el joven Asistente de enfermería me atendieron con una verdadera calidad
De servicios médicos y con un Carácter
Humano y gran profesionalismo. Felicidades a todos ellos.

José Rodolfo Alfonso

reviews.review_text:
Unfortunately, I can't give it 0 stars because it won't let me, but that would be my rating for this place. I went there to get an ultrasound of my veins because they burst, and to rule out any blood clots. I arrived around 4:20 pm, checked in, and about 10 minutes later they called me to take my blood pressure and vital signs—the routine check they do. They told me to wait outside and that they would call me for the ultrasound.

Four hours passed, and they never called me for the exam. I decided to get up and go to the front desk and tell them I was leaving because the wait was too long.
It's disrespectful to make you wait that long.
Now I'll be watching to see if they try to bill my insurance company $$$ just for taking my blood pressure.
Unfortunately, medical care in this country is incredibly bureaucratic.
reviews.original_review_text:
Lamentablemente no puedo darle 0️⃣ estrellas ⭐️ porque no me lo permite, pero esa sería mi calificación para este lugar. Llegué para que me realizaran un ultrasonido de las venas porque se me reventaron y para descartar algún cuájulo de sangre 🩸 y resulta que llegué como a las 4:20 pm, me registré y como a los 10 minutos me llamaron para tomarme la presión y signos vitales de rutina que te hacen, me dijeron espera afuera que te van a llamar para el ultrasonido.
Pasaron 4 horas y nunca me llamaron para hacerme el examen. Decidí levantarme y dirigirme al front desk y decirle que me retiraba porque era demasiado el tiempo de espera.
Es una falta de respeto que te hagan esperar tanto tiempo.
Ahora estaré pendiente a ver si pasan factura $$$ al seguro solo por tomar la presión.
Desafortunadamente la atención médica en este país es burocrática de lo peor.

reviews.review_text:
It was a terrible experience. First, I went to the urgent care clinic and told them I had pain in my left shoulder blade (scapula) and felt electric shocks in my arm and armpit. Instead of examining me, they ordered an electrocardiogram (ECG). Then they told my husband to take me to the emergency room quickly, and the same thing happened there. The doctor ordered another ECG, only to tell me it was muscular. A terrible experience.
On Monday, I went to my primary care physician, and they diagnosed me with shingles. What a terrible time and such awful pain, and they couldn't diagnose anything. If they had examined me properly, they would have found what was wrong. I'm not giving it even one star because it was a terrible experience.
reviews.original_review_text:
Fue una terrible experiencia, primero llegué al urgent care lo cual le dije que me dolía el omoplato ósea la scapula del lado izquierdo que sentía como corrientasos en el brazo y axila y en vez de revisarme dió la orden de un electrocardiograma luego le dijo a mi esposo que me lleve rápido a hospital de emergencia y lo mismo pasó en emergencia la Dra allí ordenó nuevamente un electrocardiograma para al final decirme que era muscular. Terrible experiencia
El lunes fui a mi médico primario y lo que tengo es culebrilla o shingles
Que mal momento y que dolores más terribles sin que puedan diagnosticar algo que si me hubieran revisado como se requiere hubieran encontrado realmente lo que tenía
Yo no le doy ni una estrella ya que para mí fue una experiencia terrible.

reviews.review_text: Last night I was at this center. The service was very good, but the prescriptions never arrived at the pharmacy. The next day I went back to the pharmacy, and they still hadn't sent the prescriptions. I called the center twice; it had been 24 hours and they still hadn't sent the prescriptions. I had a terrible cough and could barely speak. One of the prescriptions they sent me was for a medication that was no longer manufactured, and the other one never arrived when I called again. They told me I had to wait an hour for the prescription to arrive, as if they were sending it by ship. If it's electronic, what's the wait? I'm never going back there in my life. But now, when it comes to getting paid, they send the prescriptions quickly, even though they don't take so long. My experience was awful, even though it's a relatively new center.
reviews.original_review_text:
Anoche estuve en este centro la atención fue muy buena pero las recetas nunca llegaron a la farmacia al otro dia volví a la farmacia y todavía no habían mandados las recetas llamé en 2 ocasiones al centro que llevaba 24 horas  que no envían las recetas yo con una toz sin casi poder hablar
Una de las recetas que me enviaron ya el medicamento ni se fabricaba y la otra nunca la enviaron cuando volví a llamar
Que yo tenía que esperar una hora para que llegara la receta ni que la enviaran en barco, si eso es electrónico cuál es la espera , allí no regreso más nunca en mi vida
Pero ahora para cobrar el bill ,sino se demoran tanto si lo mandan rápido
Mi experiencia fue pésima a pesar que es un centro relativamente nuevo

reviews.review_text: It's unfortunate that this Baptist establishment is associated with Baptist Health West Kendall as the difference is night & day.  I'll start off by saying that your nurses need to go back to training to learn how to handle elderly people.  If you are elderly & can avoid this place, stay away.  You don't yank, pull, or toss an elderly person especially when they are there because of a back injury.  The lack of professionalism and empathy is off the scale.  I thought I had mistakenly visited an animal hospital at one point then it dawned on me, even those know how to show compassion.  One red flag was when I was told to wait in the patient room while they took her to get X-Rays.  They obviously didn't want me to see how they were going to treat her.  In every single hospital I've been to, I have never been refused the option of following my family member inside.  She refused medication for her pain and I am glad she did, because unless they are euthanizing someone at this place, for your safety you should avoid ANY type of drug or injection that they ask to administer.  I wouldn't be surprised if they hung you on a wall & played darts on your loved one.  When it came time to pay, I opted for being billed at home, because the ambience was so bad I wanted to avoid the possibility of a skimmer being attached to the credit card machine.  Also, what is the deal with the vulgar language that is used so nonchalantly these days?  Is that learned in med school or is it a product of the educational system of this generation?  While I waited I had the privilege of over hearing about how your laptop caught on fire at work & how you handled it.  Which to be honest I'm not surprised as this whole place is a dumpster fire.  The security guard & the lady at the front desk who put the identification bracelet on her were the only two good interactions i had.  Also the doctor Mascarinas,  even though she seemed to be in a hurry.  I totally understood though, as I couldn't wait to leave that place myself.  In closing, i will relegate this place to the trash heap of what passes as medical care these days.  Needless to say I won't be going here ever again.  Drive a little further if you can & avoid.  Your loved ones and your mental health will thank you.

This facility is phenomenal! I’ve been here several times for myself and always found it to be very clean, organized and wonderful medical staff. My mother-in-law also has been there and she was having serious medical condition for years. No one was able to find out what was wrong with her and a doctor in this facility, saved her life. They found out she was suffering from a heart condition.
Excellent service. Everyone was very professional and kind. The place is clean and organized. Highly recommend.
I felt very well taken care of and in the best hands. The nurse who attended to me was incredibly kind, as was the doctor. Excellent service.

reviews.review_text: My visit to this place wasn't very satisfactory… the place is full of trainees 😔 unfortunately, and well, it's okay, we all learn at some point, but the girl who drew my blood treated me terribly and drew the blood incorrectly (clotted), so the supervisor had to come and do it again, and then everything was even worse! The woman doing the ultrasound didn't know what she was doing, she didn't know how to operate the machine, and she couldn't find or identify anything she needed to see. The abdominal ultrasound took 40 minutes, but it was all due to sheer negligence, and then the transvaginal ultrasound took another 45 minutes. Don't even get me started on that 😔 She treated me so badly, and without a doubt, she didn't know what she was doing. She couldn't find what she needed, and I had to endure that for 45 minutes, asking the person who was there with her everything (she didn't know anything about the system she was using). It was very annoying and unfortunate because I was in a very vulnerable situation, but oh well… this is my opinion. I hope no one else has to go through what I went through 😔
reviews.original_review_text: Mi visita a este lugar no fue muy satisfactoria… el lugar está lleno de practicantes 😔 lamentablemente, y pues está bien todos aprendemos en algún momento, pero la chica al sacarme sangre me maltrató un montón y sacó la sangre mal (coabulada) así que tuvo q venir la jefe a hacerlo de nuevo y después fue peor todo! la señora del ultrasonido no sabía lo que hacía,  no sabía el manejo de la máquina, ni podía encontrar o evidenciar lo q fuera q necesitara mirar, se demoró 40 min en la abdominal pero todo por mera negligencia y se demoró luego 45 min en el ultrasonido intravaginal en este ni hablar 😔 me maltrató tanto y sin duda alguna ella no sabía lo que hacía, no podía encontrar lo q necesitaba y tuve q soportar eso 45 min le preguntaba todo a la persona q estaba allí con ella ( ella no conocía nada del sistema que estaba usando) fue muy molesto y lamentable porque yo estaba en una situación muy vulnerable, pero bueno.. esta es mi opinión espero q nadie pase lo q yo pase 😔

we all know that going into a hospital or an emergency room is never fun. It makes it easier and comforting when you have good caring, professional nurses that take care of you. starting from the front desk, when I first walked in the receptionist was awesome. She was caring. She was helpful and she was sympathetic to how I was feeling my nurse Leslie she was amazing. She explained everything she treated me with warmth and care. I had mentioned to her that my prior visit had Not been the best, specially when initiating an IV. She told me not to worry she quickly came prepared my arm, and to be honest, I didn’t even feel the pinch. She made sure I was comfortable as well as warm. Thank you, Leslie. It’s hard to find nurses that practice with your heart and enjoy what they do. Keep Up the good work, you’re an inspiration.
Thank you again
MauraMunoz

Today, I had a great experience at Baptist Health Emergency Care on Coral Way West. From the moment I arrived, the staff was kind, respectful, and very professional. The check-in process was smooth, and the wait time was reasonable.

The medical provider was attentive and took the time to listen to my concerns, explaining everything clearly. I also want to highlight the nursing team, especially the nurse leader, who was excellent. Their professionalism, calm approach, and support made a real difference in my experience and made me feel truly well cared for.

The clinic was clean, well organized, and efficiently run. Overall, the level of care was outstanding, and I would confidently recommend this location to anyone looking for prompt and compassionate medical attention.

reviews.review_text:
Excellent care. I arrived with severe respiratory distress and was examined promptly despite the wards being full. They immediately began my treatment and administered the supplements prescribed by the doctor. The entire team was excellent in their care, professionalism, and kindness. I congratulate them because, as I mentioned before, the emergency room was overflowing with cases. I want to highlight the work of nurse Roberto, who stayed with me until the very end. He explained the discharge paperwork and the prescribed home treatment, and even escorted me to my daughter's car. I commend him for his patience with each of his patients, especially considering he hadn't had time to eat, it was almost 8 pm, and his shift ended at 11 pm.
I left completely satisfied with the service I received. Happy New Year to everyone!
reviews.original_review_text:
Buena atención , llegué con un estado respiratorio muy fuerte, fui examinada sin mucha demora a pesar de estar llenos los cuertos, de inmediato comenzaron mi tratamiento y hacerme los complementos indicados por el médico , todo el equipo que estaba trabajando fue excelente en su trato , profesionalidad , y muy amables, los felicito porque como dije antes estaba lleno emergencia de casos ..: quiero destacar la labor del enfermero Roberto que estuvo hasta el último momento dándome ,nos explicó lo referente a los documentos del alta y el tratamiento indicado para la casa ,  me llevó hasta la salida llevándome a subir al carro de mi hija , lo felicito por su paciencia con cada uno de sus pacientes y sin haber tenido tiempo de comer y ya eran casi las 8 de la noche y su turno terminaba a las 11pm ;
salí satisfecha con todo del servicio recibido , a todos feliz año nuevo !!!

reviews.review_text: I would like to thank the entire emergency team for the very professional care I received. My nurse, Luis, Paul, and the amazing nurse who finally was able to find my vein using an ultrasound machine and opened a line for me; but sadly I can not remember his name, was most patient and efficient -

I went in for back pain today the nurses are amazing, the front desk receptionist are so professional and caring.  They even brought me a wheelchair so I didn’t have to walk.  I can’t stress enough how grateful I am for all the doctors and nurses that attended to me.
I would highly recommend this ER! The process was very quick from arrival to release and the staff was attentive and very helpful. Barely a wait time to even be seen.

The Baptist Health ER located on Coral Way (26 Street) and 145 Avenue delivered the best health care experience I've ever had.  The care from the entire staff exceeded my expectations - from the front desk attendant and security staff to the advanced practitioner provider, Juan Carlos Hidalgo; the amazingly caring nurse, Leslie; to the CT scan tecnician.. An impecablely clean place; seamless service from entry to exit; and even got my prescribed medication filled at their on-site vending machine. The entire process took 2 hours. This will definitely be my go to place for any future medical emergency needs. Another plus, it is very close to my house.

reviews.review_text: My son popped his lip open and my choice was Baptist emergency room as it always is and we were in and out in an hour. You did great buddy” MUCH BETTER OF A RESPONSE AND WAY TO CALM MY SON! (Thank you Julio!)

reviews.review_text: Came here because the doctor had no availability—so glad I did!! Amazing service from front desk to checking out! Think I prefer this than the doctors office lol! Thanks for the amazing service 🤩!!

If specialists agree to be placed on preferred ER specialists on call, should have sooner openings for ER follow ups.
The best emergency center, I give it 100.

reviews.review_text:
Worse customer service!   My daughter’s vitals were checked upon arrival she was found to have 101.4 fever. We were asked to sit out side until a room was available 10 min later they had us sit in the consultation room we sat there for 38 min with no MD or nurse coming by   Mind you this is not a DNV approved patient room that we were left in.  I had to go find a nurse to ask for anyone to come by as her fever was still not adresses. The ARNp then comes by as unprofessional as can be like if we are in the hood.  No stethoscope in hand to check my daughter‘s lungs esophagus, he used the light from his cell phone to look for snow caps for evidence of strep. He did not check her ears. He did not check her lungs. He did not address her fever at that moment either we continue to wait another 30 minutes when the nurse finally showed up to give my daughter Tylenol ibuprofen at this time my daughter‘s fever was ready at 103.8 why because your nurse took over an hour to address the situation all this time while being in a consultation room and other patients coming in after us that were not at the worst state than we were taking out rooms that we were entitled to. I did tell your auntie for this kind of service which is not Baptist level. Just called to Kendall Regional hospital. I was extremely disappointed on the way your staff from my nurse, especially my nurse everything she had a nasty attitude when I told her it was taking too long to give my daughter her fever medication and as I spoke to her telling her that why were we in a non-approved DMV room this entire time she just kept saying yeah yeah yeah yeah yeah walking away. The ARNP that was there was Estrada Fiego F. The rude nurse was Daniella    No MD ever came by so
I really don’t know why Viviana Robert Anthony name is on her bill nor paperwork from our visit
Super cleaning, fast attention, friendly staff. I’m so happy

As long as I can choose where to go or take my family, this location will always be my first choice. Doctors are amazing and the staff show empathy and compassion for patients.  Great experience under stressful circumstances. Thank you! 🙏

reviews.review_text: This place treats you immediately once you’re in, but the one part that really bothered me was that they need to manage time better. I recently came here because I had a kidney stone. I was in severe horrible pain and I told the person at the front desk that I urgently wanted to see a doctor and he told me I needed to fill out a form. I filled out that form and then I waited what felt like the longest 20 minutes in that waiting room suffering from the horrible pain of a kidney stone. Someone that entered after me was also let in before me. For an emergency room, time needs to be managed better. Other than that, once i finally was called in and let into one of those hospital beds, i was immediately given some fluid by injection which i assumed is to keep me hydrated. I answered some more questions and moments later the doctor ordered me to take some pain relief by injection. Like 10 minutes later the pain started going away and then i was taken to some xray machine where i assume is that they determined I had a kidney stone. Lesley, the nurse that attended me, was great she was very kind and did a great job. Give her a raise. On the other hand the people at the front desk need some more training on what an emergency room is and to attend people as soon as possible because nobody should be waiting 20 minutes in a waiting room of an emergency room while in horrible unbearable pain.

I appreciated all the staff  that yesterday assisted me at the hospital for their exellent job.

Sincerely,

Maria Gonzalez

I usually don't write reviews for anything, let alone an ER facility, but I felt compelled to do so. I was beyond impressed with my experience at Baptist Emergency in Coral Way. More importantly, the level of care and kindness from every staff member who assisted me was exceptional.

The ER doctor took the time to explain everything clearly throughout my visit, which put me at ease and made me feel cared for and supported. I left feeling not only grateful for the quality of medical attention I received, but also for the compassion shown by the entire team.

Thank you to everyone at Baptist Emergency Coral Way for making a stressful situation so much easier. I’m truly appreciative of the outstanding care.

reviews.review_text: I can’t say enough wonderful things about this Emergency department. From the moment I walked in to the moment I walked out I was listened to. I was swarmed  with a healthcare team because my because my blood pressure was sky high. The attending Dr., Dr. LaPorta was there from the moment I laid down in the bed. Her bedside manner was the best I’ve ever experienced. From the moment I sat after breaking down all of my symptoms and current diagnoses, I can see her making calculations in her head, lol. She was good because she ordered every test I needed and beyond. We found answers in every diagnostic test performed. I’m hoping the follow up testing doesn’t lead up to worse circumstances but if it does, I have her to thank for finding these problems. I can then say she saved my life. She is an asset to the Baptist team and deserves so much recognition. She is passionate about medicine and wants answers. She didn’t push away any theories I had and was open to all possibilities and that’s why she is awesome. Most doctors just shrug you away and tell you not to read so much on google. Sometimes self advocating, educated patients make a great team with doctors. The radiology tech that did my ultrasounds dove deep and took a good long time. I wish I could thank her. I’m thankful to her for doing such a wonderful job because she found things that answer so many years of questions. I wish I knew her name but it’s not on any of my paperwork. The CT tech was amazing as well. He was super sweet and calmed my nerves. I didn’t get his name either, unfortunately. Aside from my experience with them, everyone else from admitting, the phlebotomist to my nurse, Diego were amazing. They were working late on a Friday night and not a frown in sight. Everyone seems happy to do their job. I will never go anywhere else for my emergencies. I love this stand alone hospital/Emergency Room.

reviews.review_text:
I regret not going sooner.
I received the best care from the moment I arrived! I was feeling really unwell, and they attended to me immediately. In just 3 hours, they performed all the necessary tests; there was no waiting whatsoever. The place is super clean, new, and everything is in excellent condition. Most importantly, the doctor and her team were incredibly attentive. She was spot-on with the treatment, explaining in detail what was happening to me, as well as the plan to stabilize me and help me feel the improvement I experienced in a short time. Thank you to Dr. Ibis Yarde MD and her team, Luis and Yeison. 🙏🏻

reviews.review_text:
I had an extremely disappointing experience at the emergency room recently. From the moment I arrived, I felt dismissed and disrespected. The doctor who attended to me was not only patronizing but also incredibly rude. Instead of addressing my concerns with care, he spoke down to me, making me feel like I was wasting his time. When I tried to ask questions about my condition, he acted as though I was being unreasonable.

To make matters worse, some of the nurses were equally unprofessional. One in particular was openly impatient when I asked for clarification about the treatment plan. It’s disheartening to be in a vulnerable state and feel like you’re being treated like a burden rather than a patient.

I understand that emergency rooms can be busy, but basic empathy and professionalism should still be a given. Unfortunately, this experience left me feeling more anxious and unheard than when I first arrived. I hope that the staff at this hospital takes the time to reflect on how important it is to treat patients with kindness, respect, and dignity, especially when they’re in a crisis.

reviews.review_text:
The overall experience was Ok but I didn’t see much of anyone. He was tested. And he did have Influenza B. We were sent home without much information or treatment plan.

We find out after the fact that my copay was $150 because we had gone to a hospital facility not an urgent care. The diagnosis was simple, almost no patient time and a very quick doctor visit. This was one of the easiest paid gigs for Baptist. If you get urgent care service (no X-rays, monitoring, etc), you shouldn’t have to pay more just because it’s the center you chose.

reviews.review_text: Very satisfied. I arrived with my mother, who was suffering from severe back pain. From the moment we arrived at reception, we were attended to immediately with respect and kindness. Shortly after, we were taken to a ward where she was treated by extremely professional, compassionate, and caring staff. From the first person to the last, everyone who attended to us was incredibly kind. The cleanliness, comfort, and warmth of the staff were impeccable. We are truly grateful to the entire medical team, nurses, assistants, technicians, students, and receptionists. My mother received her treatment immediately, and after receiving the diagnosis, just 24 hours later, I can tell you that she has improved significantly thanks to you and to God. On behalf of myself, my mother, and our family, we are eternally grateful. Thank you for everything. Greetings and blessings.
reviews.original_review_text: Muy satisfecha, llegué con mi mamá con dolor muy fuerte de espalda desde la recepción fuimos atendida de inmediato con respeto  y amabilidad al rato nos pasaron donde fue atendida por personal extremadamente profesional , humanismo y cariño desde la primera persona hasta la última que nos atendió, la limpieza , el confort , el calor humano es impecable ,estamos verdaderamente agradecidos con todo el equipo médico , enfermeras , asistentes , técnicos y estudiantes y recepcionistas , mi mamá tuvo su tratamiento de inmediato y después de conocer el diagnóstico, hoy a solo 24 horas les puedo decir que ha mejorado mucho gracias a ustedes y al Señor  el cual a mi nombre, el de mi mamá y familia les estamos eternamente agradecidos , gracias por todo . Saludos y Bendiciones.

reviews.review_text: Nobody likes having to go to the emergency room, that is one of the last options we take especially because of the wait and in some cases because of the treatment. But this time I can say the opposite, I felt like I was at home and my mom was the one taking care of me. From the moment you enter, the people at the front desk were attentive and concerned. The RN was very delicate when it came to taking me and taking the blood samples, he was always attentive at all times, just as the doctor took his time to order me to do all the tests I needed and rule out what I had. Honestly, I have never felt as well cared for as I did thanks to all of your staff who were excellent at their work.

reviews.review_text:
BEWARE of the BILL!. This is not a normal urgent care facility, and it is not a hospital, but they bill like a hospital and if you have a deductible your insurance will not cover it! For a total of 2 hours inside checking up on a long standing medical issue I had, they sent me a bill for $2500 and a new bill for seeing the doctor for $600 totaling over $3000. They told me to email Patient Experience, which I did 6 times already, no one answers the phones and 6 emails have gone not responded to.

I came in for leg pain from an accident I had 15 years ago. The doctors did an Xray and an ultra sound, then told me there was nothing they could do and gave me a bandage wrap and told me to take basic pain medication. I was at the center for about 2 hours.

When I googled urgent care near me this location on 14591 SW 26th St came up. I scheduled the urgent care visit on the pine app before checking in. I also searched though the website for a list of hospitals and this address is not listed as a hospital. My bill for this visit came this week and it is $2512 and my insurance claims this as a hospital visit. This is much more then the Baptist website advertises. Additionally I got a new bill for seeing the doctor for $600, which is not including the 2,096.00 I was charged for “Care” on the bill.

Come for a bit if pain left with months of anxiety and debt.

reviews.review_text: The facility is great. For the most part staff is helpful and friendly. Lisa the nurse was very professional and caring at all times. The person at registration was excellent. What I have to say about this experience is something that has happened before. After the first nurse takes your vitals and asks about your condition (no complaints about him), then you are taken to a room that has many equipment, and it doesn’t look like a place for a doctor to see you. The doctor was very nice, only after I complained I was taken to a room. I don’t think this is right, first of all I didn’t feel well, and come on this visit was not for free. I understand sometimes there are many patients which was not the case, because I saw a couple of empty rooms myself. You should find a better office for the doctor to see the patients. The supervisor came and apologized for the inconveniences. The place is spotless.

The Doctor or nurse practitioner who treated me was very bad-tempered. I didn't feel like he cared about my case, which was very painful.
The nurse(s) were not very professional, they took a long time to answer a call and it seemed that they wanted to finish everything quickly, they didn't listen to the patient.
The only thing I can say is that the technician who performed the CTScan on me was very professional and a good person who cares about how the patient feels.
I have been working in a hospital for 20 years and I know when a patient doesn't feel well cared for.

reviews.review_text: I'm deeply dissapointed with the care my child received in the Emergency Room on August 13th. The Nurse Practitioner Yoarli Tapanes, did not perform a proper examination of my child. He entered the room with a mask on with a very rude demeanor, asked my 7 year old "do you have pain" , quickly looked at his ear and misdagnosed him with Otitis Externa. This misdiagnosis caused unecessesary stress and delayed proper treament to my child. We had to visit another urgent care the next day because my child spent the whole night screaming in pain, where we finally received the correct diagnosis and treatment. Both my husband and I are APRN's who work in healthcare and find it unacceptable for any medical professional to be so careless, especially when dealing with a child's health. I hope this feedback prompts a review of their procedures to ensure this doesn't happen to anyone else.

reviews.review_text: Worst experience ever. I suffer from kidney stones since my sophomore year of high school (very young) and I am now in my second year of college, still struggling with this. There’s been many ER visits through out the years and this was the worst experience by far. I was given no IV fluid for my dehydration and doctor insisted that there were no problems. They do not care about you. I understood that the Scans did not find anything, however, I still have pain. I asked what solutions can be made for this pain i’ve been experiencing which has made it difficult for me to partake in every day activities!! His solution was to keep taking pain medication! No body wants to spend their life popping pills, i came to find the problem and was told there was none! This pain is unbearable! and what do you know? It was a kidney stone as expected, which Ive passed very small pieces today. They should have seen any trace of UTI in the urine as well. I’m going to have to get antibiotics elsewhere.  Not only that but an ultra sound as well, because they didn’t bother to do that either. Do not come here. They don’t care about you and they wont listen to you!

reviews.review_text:
I have been a regular visitor to this facility and have always been pleased with the service, but my latest visit left me feeling quite disappointed.

I came to Baptist Hospital on July 12th because I had tested positive for Covid-19 on July 5th and was still feeling very unwell. During my visit, I was seen by Physician Assistant Gustavo Adolfo Martinez. Unfortunately, from the moment I met Mr. Martinez, I felt dismissed and not taken seriously. The first thing he said to me was, “but you look healthy,” which felt quite dismissive given my ongoing symptoms.

Mr. Martinez tested me for Covid-19, flu, and strep, and ordered an X-ray, all of which came back negative. I showed him a small lump by my jawline, which I believed to be a swollen lymph node and thought might require antibiotics. His response was, “stop thinking about it, get it out of your head and don’t go looking on Dr. Google because you’re going to think you have lymphoma.”

I felt that Mr. Martinez was gaslighting me by dismissing my concerns and not taking the time to explain why a swollen lymph node might occur. His approach made me feel unheard and invalidated. A simple, informative explanation would have alleviated much of my worry.

In contrast, the nurses and admitting staff were very polite and attentive, which I greatly appreciated. It is disappointing to have such a stark difference in professionalism and care between them and Mr. Martinez.

I hope you will take this feedback into consideration and address these issues to ensure that all patients feel heard, respected, and well-cared for during their visits.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.
reviews.owner_answer: Hello Nelly - We are committed to providing an excellent patient experience, so we are sorry that your expectations were not met on this visit. Thank you for sharing your feedback.

reviews.review_text:
I did not received medical attention at Baptist Urgent Care at Miller & 147 Av. and I was refered to the Baptist Emergency at Coral Way and 147 Av.
because I was told that they did not have the ability to perform a CT Scan for the pain in my right leg.
At my arrival at Baptist Emergency I told  the nurse that I been refered to them for a CT Scan. The doctor came and told me that what I need it was an MRI that had to be done at a Hospital, and the Doctor did not check my leg. I did not received any attention for my condition.
I am very disapointed.
I would like somebody with authority to call me so I can explain how I feel about these visits.
Thank you
reviews.owner_answer: Hello Iraida - Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. We truly value your feedback and will be sure to look into your concerns. If you would like to speak to a member of our Patient Experience team, please email us at PatientExperience@baptisthealth.net or call 786-596-3750.

reviews.review_text: The place is beautiful. The doctor in charge of the ER doctor, Dr.Baez is unprofessional, sarcastic, and doesn't deserve the title "doctor". First they stuck me in a closet, instead of a room. You would think they were full but no I was actually one of the few people there. The beds were empty. He kept on saying why I was there for a call. I kept on trying to explain that I have a rear autoimmune disease that affects my lungs and I needed a CT scan because I might be having a flare and if I have a flare that needs to be treated right away or I can lose lung function. He kept on saying I have nothing and all he was going to do was an x-ray. I told him no. I need a CT scan because again I have a rare autoimmune disorder. He never asked for my medical history which I have never heard of and I've gone to a lot of ERS since I am extremely immunocompromised. He never asked what my medicine list was. The whole visit was just extremely strange. At the end since I told him I needed a CT scan and I refused to leave. If I didn't get one he told me sarcastically, "well since you know more than I do"... I have never been treated so poorly. Shame on you! Needless to say, when he saw my CT results then all of a sudden his attitude changed because he noticed my lungs were messed up.
reviews.owner_answer: Hello Michelle - Thank you for making us aware of your experience, and we are sorry it was not positive. Please feel free to contact our Patient Experience team at  PatientExperience@baptisthealth.net or 786-596-3750 to discuss your concerns. Thank you.

reviews.review_text: My experience at this place was extremely unpleasant. I arrived with my wife almost fainting, not knowing what was wrong with her. The receptionist immediately dismissed her as having a virus, saying everyone had it, without even testing her. She seemed annoyed or unwilling to work because her treatment of me was completely unprofessional. I told her my wife was vomiting and asked if she could please give me a bag, and she became annoyed every time I mentioned my wife's health. She responded irritably, as if she didn't care. Her response was that everyone there felt sick, and at that moment there was only one other woman waiting. My wife was trembling with fever and vomiting, and she did nothing, showing no interest in her condition. I simply told her my wife felt ill and asked for help, and her response was that she couldn't do anything and that I had to wait. I don't know what else she wanted me to wait for. My recommendation is to avoid this place if you can. At least the receptionist was completely unprofessional, as were the other employees. I didn't see them there because, seeing their inability to treat a patient who felt unwell, I decided to go to another hospital, and it was the best decision I made. Thanks to that, my wife received the care that people should receive when they go to an emergency room.
reviews.original_review_text: Mi experiencia en este lugar fue demasiado desagradable llego con mi esposa casi desmayada ahí sin saber lo que tenía y ya la señora de la recepción decía que era un virus que eso lo tenía todo el mundo restándole importancia sin ni hacerle un análisis al parecer ella estaba molesta o no tenía ganas de trabajar porque el trato que me brindó no lo sentí nada profesional además de eso le comento que mi esposa estaba vomitando que si por favor me podía dar una bolsa y también se molestó cada ves que le decía o comentaba algo con respecto a la salud de mi esposa ella me respondía como molesta como si lo que le estaba diciendo no le importaba su respuesta fue que todo el mundo ahí se siente mal y en ese momento sólo había una señora esperando no había más nadie  mientras mi mujer estaba temblando con fiebre y vómitos ahí no hizo nada ni mostró interés por su estado de salud sólo le dije que se sentía mal que me ayudara y su respuesta fue que no podía hacer nada que tenía que esperar no sé qué más queria que esperara  mi recomendación es que no vayan ahí si pueden evitarlo por lo menos la señora de recepción nada profesional los otros trabajadores de ahí no los vi ya que al ver la incapacidad que tenían para tratar a un paciente que se sentía en malas condiciones decidí ir a otro hospital y fue la mejor decisión que tome  gracias a eso a mi esposa la atendieron como se deben atender a las personas cuando van a un hospital de urgencias

reviews.review_text: My 9 year old went here with his papa and they had tested him for everything, he had fever head throwing up and a super bad cough. Strep clear, Covid clear, flu clear. Common cold for what they said. Went to his PCP to follow up after discharge and dr. Said hey if In 3 he doesn’t get any better take him back I didn’t wait the 3rd day, took him back his fevers got to 103, this time I took him again strep, covid, flu clear. I asked to check urine to see if he had an infection, after that was also negative, I asked please do a Xray I know this is something more. Mind you I’m a nurse for 18 years so I know when there is something wrong. The doctor said let’s do it. Let me tell you my baby had pneumonia. Thank you to all the staff and doctors for making his stay comfortable him being a special boy they made sure he felt safe and trusted them. And he sure did. It’s been 3 weeks and he is way better. I highly recommend this place.just a question is this on admission hospital? Or more like observation? Like if need to be observed 24/72 hour is that possible?

reviews.review_text:
January 5, 2024

As a long-time patron of Baptist facilities, I am compelled to share our recent appalling experience at their emergency room, which starkly contrasted with the high standards we've come to expect.

My fiancé and I returned from Europe celebrating New Year's when he developed severe flu-like symptoms. Given our past positive experiences with Baptist, it was our immediate choice for care. However, the care we received this time was deeply disappointing.

Upon arrival, my fiancé was in extreme distress, suffering from continuous vomiting, severe coughing, and chest pains to the point of being unable to speak or breathe properly. To our dismay, the attending doctor displayed a complete lack of empathy and professionalism. When my fiancé, in sheer agony, gestured for me to speak on his behalf, the doctor brusquely insisted that he must speak for himself. This insensitivity was not only shocking but also compounded our stress in an already tense situation.

The inefficiencies didn't end there. We endured a frustrating wait of over four hours for his lab results. Despite our queries, we received no updates or explanations for the delay. This lack of communication was not only unprofessional but also left us feeling neglected and anxious.

Moreover, throughout this ordeal, my fiancé was in excruciating pain, often screaming, yet the response to provide pain relief was unacceptably slow. When the results finally arrived, they were dismissively reported as 'normal,' contradicting the visible and severe symptoms he was experiencing. This dismissal of his pain and suffering was not only demoralizing but also led us to question the thoroughness of the evaluation.

As I write this, we find ourselves seeking care at another hospital due to the persistence of his symptoms. It is disheartening and alarming that an institution we once trusted has failed us so profoundly.

My advice: reconsider visiting this Baptist emergency room. Our experience was akin to that of a subpar urgent care, marked by rude staff and inadequate medical attention. This is not the standard of emergency care anyone should expect, especially in critical times of need.
reviews.owner_answer: Hello Natasha - We are sorry you had this experience. We value your feedback and will share your comments with our team. If you would like to further discuss your concerns, please contact our Patient Experience team at 786-596-3750 or PatientExperience@baptisthealth.net. Thank you so much.

reviews.review_text:
I don’t recommend going here. They take super long to give you their results and no one is around . It wasn’t even busy and they ignore you when you call out for the nurse . it’s very expensive. I wanted to go to an urgent care but they were all closed by 9pm. this place is open 24 hours. If you have an emergency just go to a hospital. Their charges are at hospital cost and you’re not getting the service as in a hospital facility. I gave them 2 stars because front desk and staff was very polite and then you’re so disappointed. They even forgot to electronically prescribe 2 medications out of 3 the doctor said he was prescribing.
Like I said , they weren’t even busy. I got in right away but they held me in a super cold room for 2hours and 30 minutes.

reviews.review_text: Some of the staff(nurses) showed little patience for my mom, Lucia. She was a little agitated and asked how long it would be. Instead of placating her and putting her at ease, the nurse told her that she was not the only patient and that the technician for the sonogram would come when it was her turn, the another nurse walked by in front of my mom’s room and made a comment how some people   My mother is 80 years old and has suffered two strokes that have altered her personality. When I explained this to the two nurses, the second nurse shook her head as I walked away as observed by my daughter. I just think they need to take a moment and step into someone else’s shoes before they judge. They don’t know that my father is bedridden with a feeding tube and cannot communicate, and she is his main caregiver. This weighs heavily on her. It takes less energy to be kind and understanding. Everyone else we encountered were very friendly. That’s why I put 4  stars.

reviews.review_text:
★☆☆☆☆

/---------------- DO NOT GO HERE ----------------/
My visit to Baptist Hospital Urgent Care left me astounded and deeply dissatisfied. Despite having insurance coverage, I was appalled by the exorbitant bill I received. My insurance, which supposedly covered everything, was billed an outrageous $15,000 for just an hour-long visit that involved a single CT scan.

To make matters worse, the care provided was subpar. The doctor seemed clueless, displaying a lack of knowledge that left me feeling uncertain and uneasy. Spending an hour there only to undergo a CT scan, without any substantial medical attention or explanation, was beyond disappointing.

The sheer audacity to charge such an astronomical amount for minimal service is unjustifiable. Additionally, the extra bill I’m now expected to cover out of my own pocket adds insult to injury. This experience has left me deeply dissatisfied and questioning the integrity of Baptist Hospital Urgent Care's practices.

I strongly caution anyone considering this facility to think twice before trusting their medical needs to a place that not only lacks competent medical professionals but also shamelessly overcharges for minimal services.

DO NOT GO THERE.
